title: LLM decoder survivor prompt-stress sweep
hypothesis: The decoder approximation paths that survived the broader distribution robustness sweep should be stress-tested across a larger prompt set before choosing a narrower hardware-oriented probability path.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Across a larger prompt-stress sample set, do the surviving integer-logit, fp probability, and approximate PWL softmax paths preserve next-token and top-k rank?
include: 24 prompt-stress samples covering factual, arithmetic, list, code-like, dialogue, punctuation, negation, units, symbolic, markup, long-context, repetition, ambiguous, enum-like, and low-context prompts
boundary points for q4 logits and fp8_e5m2 final probability
exclude: already-collapsed fixed probability q8 and fp8_e4m3 paths
follow_on_broad_sweep: add model/weight distribution diversity once more decoder artifacts exist
carry the surviving PWL and probability paths into hardware cost estimation

expected_benefit: checks that the previously surviving paths were not artifacts of the 12-row distribution set
keeps the next evaluator job bounded while broadening prompt coverage
identifies which survivor family should move toward implementation-cost work
risks: the prompt-stress set still uses the same tiny ONNX model and tokenizer
expected continuations are labels only; rank comparison is based on model reference output
software-emulated quantization remains an approximation of future hardware
